required_skills: Core game design including game loop architecture, player motivation models, and progression system design
Game design documentation including Game Design Documents (GDD), feature specifications, flowcharts, and system diagrams
Systems design covering economy balancing, meta-game progression, ability systems, and reward loop engineering
Level design fundamentals including spatial flow, pacing, encounter design, and readability principles
Playtesting methodology including structured test session design, data collection, and evidence-based design iteration
Proficiency with game engine editor tools for rapid prototyping and in-engine feature implementation without programmer dependency
Quantitative and qualitative player data analysis to evaluate feature performance and inform design decisions
Cross-disciplinary collaboration with engineers, artists, and narrative teams to deliver cohesive feature implementations
preferred_skills: Combat and encounter design for action or RPG games including enemy behavior scripting, difficulty tuning, and arena design
Monetization and live-ops design including battle pass systems, limited-time event design, and free-to-play economy balancing
Narrative design integration including branching dialogue systems, environmental storytelling, and lore system architecture
User interface and HUD design principles for communicating game state, tutorials, and accessibility options to players
Data-driven design using A/B testing, funnel analysis, and retention metric interpretation from analytics platforms
Multiplayer and social system design including matchmaking philosophy, social features, and competitive ranking systems
Shipped title experience with a demonstrable portfolio of designed systems or features in a production game context
responsibilities: Conceive, document, and champion core gameplay systems including combat mechanics, progression loops, ability frameworks, and player economy models from concept through shipping
Author detailed Game Design Documents (GDDs) and feature specification sheets that provide engineering, art, and QA teams with unambiguous implementation guidance
Prototype and iterate on new gameplay features rapidly using Blueprint scripting or Unity Visual Scripting, demonstrating intended behavior before full engineering implementation
Design and balance player progression systems including XP curves, reward schedules, unlock trees, and economy sinks to achieve target engagement and retention metrics
Plan and facilitate structured playtesting sessions, synthesizing feedback into actionable design revisions with clear prioritization rationale
Collaborate with combat programmers to design enemy AI behaviors, difficulty tuning parameters, and encounter compositions that deliver intended player challenge and satisfaction
Define and track design KPIs including session length, feature engagement rates, difficulty drop-off points, and retention curves using analytics tools and player telemetry
Design live-ops seasonal content and limited-time events including reward structures, progression paths, and replayability hooks that re-engage both new and returning players
Work with UI/UX designers to ensure game systems are communicated clearly to players through HUD elements, tutorials, onboarding flows, and contextual in-game messaging
Conduct competitive analysis of peer titles, documenting best-in-class design patterns, monetization models, and feature benchmarks to inform the product's design evolution
